fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Texas Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Texas - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity texas, how to transfer occupational therapist license to texas, occupational therapist compact texas, texas occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Texas Executive Council of PT and OT Examiners (ECPTOTE)
board.parent_dept: Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) exam

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Texas.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Register for Online Licensing Account
steps.description: Create an account on the Texas Online Licensing System (online.ptot.texas.gov) to begin your application process.

steps.title: Complete Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Pass the Texas OT Jurisprudence Examination, which is required for all applicants. Information on accessing the exam is available on the PTOT website or within the application on the Texas Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log into your account on the Texas Online Licensing System and complete the online application for an Occupational Therapist-Registered (OTR) license, selecting 'New License'.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for your official occupational therapy education transcripts and your official NBCOT score report to be sent directly to the Texas Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners. Ensure all other required documents are uploaded or mailed as specified in your Texas Online Licensing System application.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the board to complete the fingerprinting process for your criminal history record check.

steps.title: Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: Once all application materials, fees, and background check results are received and verified, the board will review your application and issue your license.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBCOT exam. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Texas OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around  to . Check the specific fee breakdown.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Texas have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Texas has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Texas?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Occupational therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current NBCOT certification.

hero.title: Become a Occupational Therapist in Texas
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Texas, you must use the Texas Board of Occupational Therapy Examiners' Texas Online Licensing System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: To create an account, visit the Texas Online Licensing System URL, click 'Register a New Account', and follow the prompts to enter your personal information and establish login credentials. Once registered, you can log in to begin a new application.
